cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-920 - Improper Restriction of Power Consumption

threat.title: gRPC Stream Flooding Attack via HTTP/2 Rapid Reset
threat.description: gRPC services built on HTTP/2 are vulnerable to stream flooding attacks where attackers rapidly create and reset streams, overwhelming server resources. The HTTP/2 Rapid Reset vulnerability (CVE-2023-44487) allows attackers to send RST_STREAM frames immediately after creating streams, causing resource exhaustion without proper rate limiting. Without MaxConcurrentStreams configuration, bidirectional streaming RPCs, large message flooding, and keepalive abuse can lead to complete service unavailability.

versions_affected: All versions using HTTP/2 without proper stream limits

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies gRPC service endpoint (typically port 50051 or 443 with TLS)
2. Establishes HTTP/2 connection with server
3. Rapidly opens thousands of concurrent streams using HEADERS frames
4. Immediately sends RST_STREAM frames to reset each stream (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset)
5. Server allocates resources for each stream but never fully processes them
6. Repeats cycle at ~400-1000 requests per second per connection
7. Server exhausts memory, CPU, and file descriptors
8. Legitimate client requests timeout or fail with UNAVAILABLE errors
9. Service becomes completely unresponsive (398 million RPS recorded in October 2023 attacks)
10. Attacker can maintain attack using minimal bandwidth due to RST_STREAM efficiency
prerequisites: No connection or stream rate limiting
No resource quotas for bidirectional streaming
Exposed gRPC endpoint (public internet or internal network access)

cve_references.description: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack - vulnerability in HTTP/2 protocol allowing rapid stream creation and cancellation to cause DoS
cve_references.affected_products: All HTTP/2 implementations including gRPC servers (grpc-go, grpc-java, grpcio)

cve_references.description: golang.org/x/net/http2: rapid stream resets can cause excessive work - specific Go implementation vulnerability
HTTP/2 Ping Flood - excessive ping frames cause denial of service
HTTP/2 Empty Frames Flood - multiple empty frames cause resource consumption

real_world_breaches.incident: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Global Attack Wave
real_world_breaches.impact: Record-breaking 398 million RPS DDoS attack against Google Cloud, Cloudflare, AWS. Multiple service outages across major cloud providers.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: CVE-2023-44487 HTTP/2 Rapid Reset vulnerability exploited at scale using RST_STREAM flooding

real_world_breaches.impact: Mitigated attacks exceeding 201 million RPS (3x larger than previous record). Peak attack lasted 65 seconds.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attackers exploited HTTP/2 multiplexing with rapid stream resets on gRPC and web services

real_world_breaches.impact: 155 million RPS attack against AWS customers. Multiple availability zones experienced degraded performance.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: CVE-2023-44487 exploited against Application Load Balancers and gRPC endpoints

real_world_breaches.impact: Cloud Run and GKE services experienced 4-hour outage affecting thousands of customers
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Uncontrolled bidirectional gRPC streams exhausted server connection pools

bug_bounty_reports.summary: gRPC service DoS via unlimited concurrent streams - 5000 streams crashed production API gateway in 12 seconds
HTTP/2 HPACK bomb attack against gRPC-web proxy caused 8GB memory consumption and OOM crash

security_research.title: HTTP/2: The Sequel is Always Worse
security_research.key_finding: HTTP/2 multiplexing creates novel DoS attack surface - single connection can exhaust server resources via stream flooding

security_research.title: gRPC Security Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: MaxConcurrentStreams must be configured - default unlimited streams allow trivial DoS attacks

mitigations.title: Configure MaxConcurrentStreams in grpc-go Server
mitigations.description: Limit the maximum number of concurrent streams per HTTP/2 connection to prevent stream exhaustion attacks. This is the most critical defense against HTTP/2 Rapid Reset and stream flooding.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Verify MaxConcurrentStreams is set (use grpcurl to test stream limits)
Test with h2load to simulate rapid stream creation: h2load -n 10000 -c 100 -m 1000 https://localhost:50051
Monitor metrics: grpc_server_streams_created_total should plateau at configured limit
Confirm RST_STREAM frames are handled without resource exhaustion
Load test with vegeta: echo 'POST https://localhost:50051/service.Method' | vegeta attack -rate=1000/s -duration=60s

mitigations.title: Implement Node.js gRPC Server Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: For Node.js @grpc/grpc-js servers, configure HTTP/2 session settings and implement connection-level rate limiting to prevent stream flooding.
validation_steps: Install dependencies: npm install @grpc/grpc-js limiter
Test rate limiting with ghz: ghz --insecure --proto service.proto --call package.Service/Method -n 10000 -c 100 localhost:50051
Monitor memory usage during stream floods
Verify RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED errors are returned when limits exceeded
Check rate limiter map size stays bounded (< 10000 entries)

mitigations.title: Deploy Envoy Proxy with HTTP/2 Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Use Envoy proxy as a gRPC gateway with advanced rate limiting, stream limits, and circuit breaking to protect backend gRPC services from DoS attacks.

code_example.code:
# envoy-grpc-protection.yaml
static_resources:
  listeners:
  - name: grpc_listener
    address:
      socket_address:
        address: 0.0.0.0
        port_value: 8080
    
    filter_chains:
    - filters:
      - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
        typed_config:
          '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager
          stat_prefix: grpc_ingress
          codec_type: AUTO
          
          # HTTP/2 DoS protection settings (CRITICAL)
          http2_protocol_options:
            max_concurrent_streams: 100              # Limit streams per connection
            initial_stream_window_size: 65536        # 64KB per stream
            initial_connection_window_size: 1048576  # 1MB per connection
            max_inbound_window_update_frames_per_data_frame_sent: 10
            stream_error_on_invalid_http_message: true
            
            # HTTP/2 Rapid Reset specific protections
            override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message: true
            max_outbound_frames: 10000
            max_outbound_control_frames: 1000
            max_consecutive_inbound_frames_with_empty_payload: 1
            max_inbound_priority_frames_per_stream: 100
          
          # Connection limits
          common_http_protocol_options:
            max_requests_per_connection: 10000
            max_connection_duration: 1800s  # 30 minutes
            idle_timeout: 300s              # 5 minutes
            headers_with_underscores_action: REJECT_REQUEST
            max_headers_count: 100
            max_stream_duration: 0s         # No limit per stream
          
          route_config:
            name: grpc_route
            virtual_hosts:
            - name: grpc_service
              domains: ["*"]
              
              # Per-route rate limiting
              rate_limits:
              - actions:
                - request_headers:
                    header_name: ":path"
                    descriptor_key: "path"
                - remote_address: {}
              
              routes:
              - match:
                  prefix: "/"
                  grpc: {}
                route:
                  cluster: grpc_backend
                  timeout: 30s
                  idle_timeout: 60s
                  
                  # Per-route retry and circuit breaking
                  retry_policy:
                    retry_on: "5xx"
                    num_retries: 3
                    per_try_timeout: 10s
          
          http_filters:
          # Rate limiting filter (must be before router)
          - name: envoy.filters.http.ratelimit
            typed_config:
              '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ratelimit.v3.RateLimit
              domain: grpc_ratelimit
              failure_mode_deny: true  # Block requests if rate limit service is down
              rate_limit_service:
                grpc_service:
                  envoy_grpc:
                    cluster_name: ratelimit_service
                transport_api_version: V3
          
          # gRPC Web filter (if using gRPC-Web)
          - name: envoy.filters.http.grpc_web
            typed_config:
              '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.grpc_web.v3.GrpcWeb
          
          # Health check filter
          - name: envoy.filters.http.health_check
            typed_config:
              '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.health_check.v3.HealthCheck
              pass_through_mode: false
              headers:
              - name: ":path"
                string_match:
                  exact: "/healthz"
          
          # Router filter (must be last)
          - name: envoy.filters.http.router
            typed_config:
              '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
  
  clusters:
  # Backend gRPC service
  - name: grpc_backend
    connect_timeout: 5s
    type: STRICT_DNS
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    
    # Circuit breaker settings (CRITICAL for DoS protection)
    circuit_breakers:
      thresholds:
      - priority: DEFAULT
        max_connections: 1000         # Max connections to backend
        max_pending_requests: 1000    # Max queued requests
        max_requests: 1000            # Max active requests
        max_retries: 3
        track_remaining: true
    
    # Connection pool settings
    upstream_connection_options:
      tcp_keepalive:
        keepalive_time: 300
        keepalive_interval: 75
        keepalive_probes: 9
    
    # HTTP/2 settings for upstream connection
    http2_protocol_options:
      max_concurrent_streams: 100
      initial_stream_window_size: 65536
      initial_connection_window_size: 1048576
    
    # Outlier detection (remove unhealthy backends)
    outlier_detection:
      consecutive_5xx: 5
      interval: 10s
      base_ejection_time: 30s
      max_ejection_percent: 50
      enforcing_consecutive_5xx: 100
    
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: grpc_backend
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: grpc-service.default.svc.cluster.local
                port_value: 50051
  
  # Rate limit service cluster
  - name: ratelimit_service
    connect_timeout: 1s
    type: STRICT_DNS
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    http2_protocol_options: {}
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: ratelimit_service
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: ratelimit.default.svc.cluster.local
                port_value: 8081

admin:
  address:
    socket_address:
      address: 0.0.0.0
      port_value: 9901

# Layered overload manager (prevents Envoy itself from being overwhelmed)
layered_runtime:
  layers:
  - name: static_layer
    static_layer:
      overload:
        global_downstream_max_connections: 50000

overload_manager:
  refresh_interval: 0.25s
  resource_monitors:
  - name: "envoy.resource_monitors.fixed_heap"
    typed_config:
      '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.resource_monitors.fixed_heap.v3.FixedHeapConfig
      max_heap_size_bytes: 2147483648  # 2GB
  
  actions:
  - name: "envoy.overload_actions.shrink_heap"
    triggers:
    - name: "envoy.resource_monitors.fixed_heap"
      threshold:
        value: 0.95
  
  - name: "envoy.overload_actions.stop_accepting_requests"
    triggers:
    - name: "envoy.resource_monitors.fixed_heap"
      threshold:
        value: 0.98

nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.AE-1 - A baseline of network operations is established
validation_steps: Deploy Envoy with config: envoy -c envoy-grpc-protection.yaml
Test stream limits: grpcurl -max-msg-sz 5242880 -d @ localhost:8080 service.Method
Simulate HTTP/2 rapid reset: h2load -n 100000 -c 1000 -m 100 http://localhost:8080
Verify circuit breaker triggers: check Envoy stats at http://localhost:9901/stats
Monitor overload manager: curl localhost:9901/stats | grep overload
Test rate limiting: ghz --connections=100 --rps=10000 localhost:8080

mitigations.title: Implement Kubernetes Resource Quotas and Pod Disruption Budgets
mitigations.description: Apply Kubernetes resource limits, HorizontalPodAutoscaler, and PodDisruptionBudgets to ensure gRPC services can scale under load and maintain availability during attacks.

code_example.code:
# grpc-service-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: grpc-service
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: grpc-service
    tier: backend
spec:
  replicas: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grpc-service
  
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: grpc-service
      annotations:
        prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
        prometheus.io/port: "9090"
    
    spec:
      # Anti-affinity to spread pods across nodes
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - weight: 100
            podAffinityTerm:
              labelSelector:
                matchLabels:
                  app: grpc-service
              topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
      
      containers:
      - name: grpc-server
        image: your-registry/grpc-service:v1.2.3
        
        # Resource limits (CRITICAL - prevents resource exhaustion)
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m       # 0.5 CPU cores
            memory: 512Mi   # 512 MB
          limits:
            cpu: 2000m      # 2 CPU cores max
            memory: 2Gi     # 2 GB max
        
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 50051
          protocol: TCP
        - name: metrics
          containerPort: 9090
          protocol: TCP
        
        # Liveness and readiness probes
        livenessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/grpc_health_probe
            - -addr=:50051
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
        
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/grpc_health_probe
            - -addr=:50051
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
          timeoutSeconds: 3
          failureThreshold: 2
        
        # Security context
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 1000
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
        
        env:
        - name: GOMAXPROCS
          valueFrom:
            resourceFieldRef:
              resource: limits.cpu
        - name: GOMEMLIMIT
          valueFrom:
            resourceFieldRef:
              resource: limits.memory
        - name: GRPC_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS
          value: "100"
        - name: GRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME_MS
          value: "7200000"
        - name: GRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIMEOUT_MS
          value: "20000"

---
# HorizontalPodAutoscaler for auto-scaling under load
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: grpc-service-hpa
  namespace: production
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: grpc-service
  
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 20
  
  metrics:
  # CPU-based scaling
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
  
  # Memory-based scaling
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: memory
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 80
  
  # Custom metric: gRPC request rate
  - type: Pods
    pods:
      metric:
        name: grpc_server_handled_total
      target:
        type: AverageValue
        averageValue: "1000"  # Scale when > 1000 RPS per pod
  
  behavior:
    scaleDown:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 300  # Wait 5min before scaling down
      policies:
      - type: Percent
        value: 50
        periodSeconds: 60
    scaleUp:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 60   # Scale up quickly
      policies:
      - type: Percent
        value: 100
        periodSeconds: 30
      - type: Pods
        value: 4
        periodSeconds: 30
      selectPolicy: Max

---
# PodDisruptionBudget to maintain availability
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: grpc-service-pdb
  namespace: production
spec:
  minAvailable: 2  # Always keep at least 2 pods running
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grpc-service

---
# NetworkPolicy to restrict traffic
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: grpc-service-netpol
  namespace: production
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grpc-service
  
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress
  
  ingress:
  # Only allow traffic from Envoy proxy
  - from:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          name: ingress
    - podSelector:
        matchLabels:
          app: envoy-proxy
    ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 50051
  
  egress:
  # Allow DNS and backend database only
  - to:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          name: kube-system
    ports:
    - protocol: UDP
      port: 53
  - to:
    - podSelector:
        matchLabels:
          app: postgres
    ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 5432

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.IP-11 - Backups of information are conducted
RC.RP-1 - Recovery plan is executed during or after an event
validation_steps: Apply manifests: kubectl apply -f grpc-service-deployment.yaml
Verify HPA: kubectl get hpa grpc-service-hpa -n production --watch
Test scaling: kubectl run -it --rm load-generator --image=busybox --restart=Never -- /bin/sh
Check PDB: kubectl get pdb grpc-service-pdb -n production
Simulate node failure: kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets
Verify no service interruption during rolling updates

indicators: Sudden spike in HTTP/2 RST_STREAM frames (> 1000/sec per connection)
Abnormally high concurrent stream count (> configured MaxConcurrentStreams)
Rapid increase in GOAWAY frames from server
Memory consumption growing linearly with connection count
CPU utilization spikes with low actual request processing
Increased UNAVAILABLE or RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED gRPC status codes
File descriptor exhaustion on gRPC server hosts
Connection pool saturation in upstream services
High ratio of stream cancellations to completions

log_queries.query:
// Detect HTTP/2 Rapid Reset attack pattern
let threshold = 1000; // RST frames per second
AzureDiagnostics
| where Category == "ApplicationGatewayFirewallLog" or Category == "ApplicationGatewayAccessLog"
| where requestUri_s contains "grpc" or httpVersion_s == "HTTP/2.0"
| extend resetFrames = toint(customDimensions.http2_rst_stream_count)
| where isnotempty(resetFrames)
| summarize 
    TotalResets = sum(resetFrames),
    UniqueClients = dcount(clientIP_s),
    AvgResetsPerSecond = sum(resetFrames) / count()
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1m), clientIP_s
| where AvgResetsPerSecond > threshold
| project TimeGenerated, clientIP_s, TotalResets, AvgResetsPerSecond, UniqueClients
| order by TotalResets desc

index=grpc_logs sourcetype=envoy_access_log
| rex field=_raw "grpc_status=(?<grpc_status>\\d+)"
| rex field=_raw "stream_id=(?<stream_id>\\d+)"
| rex field=_raw "duration=(?<duration>\\d+)ms"
| eval is_rapid_reset=if(duration < 100 AND grpc_status=="0", 1, 0)
| stats 
    count as total_requests,
    sum(is_rapid_reset) as rapid_resets,
    dc(stream_id) as unique_streams,
    avg(duration) as avg_duration_ms
    by client_ip, _time
| eval reset_ratio=rapid_resets/total_requests
| where reset_ratio > 0.7 OR rapid_resets > 1000
| eval severity=case(
    rapid_resets > 10000, "critical",
    rapid_resets > 5000, "high",
    rapid_resets > 1000, "medium",
    1=1, "low")
| table _time, client_ip, total_requests, rapid_resets, reset_ratio, avg_duration_ms, severity
| sort - rapid_resets

// Detect gRPC stream flooding via concurrent stream count
event.dataset: "grpc.server" AND grpc.status_code: (14 OR 8 OR 4)
| stats 
    count() as error_count,
    cardinality(grpc.stream_id) as concurrent_streams,
    avg(event.duration) as avg_duration_ns
    by source.ip, @timestamp
| where concurrent_streams > 100 OR error_count > 500
| eval attack_confidence = case(
    concurrent_streams > 500 AND error_count > 1000, "high",
    concurrent_streams > 200 OR error_count > 500, "medium",
    true, "low"
  )
| sort -error_count

# Detect HTTP/2 DoS attacks on AWS ALB with gRPC
fields @timestamp, @message, client_ip, target_status_code, request_processing_time
| filter target_status_code = "503" or target_status_code = "504"
| filter request_processing_time > 0 and request_processing_time < 0.1
| parse @message /(?<protocol>HTTP\/\\d\.\\d)/
| filter protocol = "HTTP/2.0"
| stats 
    count(*) as request_count,
    count_distinct(client_ip) as unique_clients,
    avg(request_processing_time) as avg_processing_time,
    pct(request_processing_time, 95) as p95_processing_time
    by bin(1m) as time_window, client_ip
| filter request_count > 1000 and avg_processing_time < 0.05
| sort request_count desc

# Alert on excessive concurrent gRPC streams
rate(grpc_server_started_total[1m]) > 1000
OR
grpc_server_handled_total{grpc_code="resource_exhausted"} > 100
OR
rate(http2_reset_stream_total[30s]) > 500
OR
(
  sum(rate(grpc_server_started_total[1m])) by (instance)
  /
  sum(rate(grpc_server_handled_total[1m])) by (instance)
) > 10  # More streams started than completed indicates flooding

tools.name: Wireshark with HTTP/2 dissector
tools.usage: wireshark -i eth0 -f 'tcp port 50051' -Y 'http2.type == 3' # Filter RST_STREAM frames

tools.name: grpcurl
tools.usage: grpcurl -v -max-msg-sz 10485760 localhost:50051 list # Test with large message size
